reason: The text contains a prophecy delivered by the prophetess Huldah (2 Kings 22:15-20) in which she declares on behalf of Yahweh: 'Behold, I am bringing evil/disaster upon this place (המקום הזה) and upon its inhabitants (יושביו)' (וְעַל־ יֹֽשְׁבָ֑יו). The prophecy specifies that this destruction is forthcoming ('הִנְנִ֨י מֵבִ֥יא' - I am bringing), supernatural in origin (from Yahweh), and explicitly affects the collective public ('this place and upon its inhabitants') rather than individual persons. The phrase 'all the words of the book' reinforces that the disaster encompasses the full scope of judgment mentioned in the discovered Book of the Covenant.
